title: The Personality Quiz Mr. Pan Made Us Take

sentences.en: Mr. Pan handed out a strange worksheet today: a list of adjectives, and we had to circle the ones that describe ourselves.
sentences.zh: 潘老師今天發了一張奇怪的學習單：一堆形容詞清單，我們要圈出能描述自己的字。

sentences.en: I circled "careful," "serious," and "friendly," though Bernice immediately said I forgot the most obvious one: "dramatic."
sentences.zh: 我圈了「小心的」、「認真的」跟「友善的」，但 Bernice 馬上說我漏掉了最明顯的一個：「戲劇化的」。

sentences.en: Bernice circled "crazy," "popular," and "lovely," and honestly, I could not really argue with any of those three.
sentences.zh: Bernice 圈了「瘋狂的」、「受歡迎的」跟「迷人的」，老實說，這三個我還真沒辦法反駁。

sentences.en: A boy near us circled "lazy" and "comfortable," and admitted his life goal was simply to be as comfortable as possible at all times.
sentences.zh: 我們附近有個男生圈了「懶惰的」跟「舒適的」，還坦承他人生目標就是隨時盡量舒適。

sentences.en: One girl circled "tall" and "strong" because she plays on the school basketball team and is proud of both.
sentences.zh: 有個女生圈了「高的」跟「強壯的」，因為她是校籃球隊的，對這兩點都很自豪。

sentences.en: Mr. Pan then asked us to guess which adjectives our classmates would circle for us, which turned out to be much harder than expected.
sentences.zh: 接著潘老師要我們猜同學會幫我們圈哪些形容詞，結果比想像中難得多。

sentences.en: Bernice guessed I would circle "stupid" for her as a joke, but I actually wrote "helpful," since she really is, in her own chaotic way.
sentences.zh: Bernice 猜我會開玩笑幫她圈「愚蠢的」，但我其實寫了「樂於助人的」，因為她真的是，只是用她自己那種混亂的方式。

sentences.en: For William, if he had to fill out this worksheet, I am fairly certain he would circle "cool," "exciting," and possibly "wonderful," all about himself.
sentences.zh: 如果 William 也要填這張學習單，我很確定他會幫自己圈「酷的」、「令人興奮的」，說不定還有「精彩的」。

sentences.en: Mr. Pan said it is a common mistake to think we are either completely one thing or completely its opposite, when really most people are a mix.
sentences.zh: 潘老師說常見的錯誤是以為自己絕對是某一種樣子或完全相反的樣子，但其實大多數人都是混合的。

sentences.en: He said someone can be shy and popular at the same time, or serious and friendly at the same time, and none of it is a contradiction.
sentences.zh: 他說一個人可以同時害羞又受歡迎，也可以同時認真又友善，這一點都不矛盾。

sentences.en: One quiet boy in the back circled "nice" and "quiet" for himself, and the whole class agreed those two words fit him perfectly.
sentences.zh: 後排一個安靜的男生幫自己圈了「好的」跟「安靜的」，全班都同意這兩個字非常適合他。

sentences.en: Bernice raised her hand and asked if it was possible to be "cheap" and "generous" at the same time, which made Mr. Pan pause for a second.
sentences.zh: Bernice 舉手問，一個人有沒有可能同時「小氣的」又「大方的」，這讓潘老師愣了一下。

sentences.en: He said it was possible, actually, if you are cheap about small things but very generous about the things you truly care about.
sentences.zh: 他說其實有可能，如果你在小事情上小氣，但對自己真正在乎的事情卻很大方。

sentences.en: That description fits my dad perfectly: he refuses to spend extra money on a nicer umbrella, but he never says no to buying a book for me.
sentences.zh: 這個描述完全就是我爸：他不肯多花錢買一把好一點的傘，但要買書給我，他從來不說不。

sentences.en: At the end of class, Mr. Pan asked us to keep this worksheet in our diaries and check it again at the end of the year to see what changed.
sentences.zh: 課堂結束時，潘老師要我們把這張學習單留在日記裡，等學年結束再回頭看看有什麼改變了。

sentences.en: I think that is a genuinely fine idea, even if I am a little afraid of what a whole year might actually change about me.
sentences.zh: 我覺得這其實是個很不錯的主意，雖然我有點害怕整整一年下來，我到底會被改變多少。

quiz.question: What adjectives does Bernice say Coco forgot to circle for herself?
According to Mr. Pan, is it a contradiction to be shy and popular at the same time?
options: No, most people are a mix of traits.
Only if you are also serious.

quiz.question: What does Coco say her dad is cheap about, but generous about books?
